_meta.description: Vertical explorer — all 13 AI/semiconductor/robotics/optical sub-chains with full step-by-step detail, chokepoint flags, and cross-chain links. Ex-materials (raw materials covered by the Hard Assets map). Fast-moving — enrich over time.

flags.choke: supply chokepoint — concentrated control of a stage

eda.note: The head of every silicon chain — highest-margin, most defensible layer. US duopoly (Synopsys + Cadence) plus Arm IP gate virtually all advanced design. Proven to be a fast on/off geopolitical lever: BIS halted EDA exports to China in May 2025, reversed ~July 2025 in a rare-earth-for-tools trade. China (Empyrean) is far behind on leading-edge full-flow.

chain.d: Synopsys (SNPS), Cadence (CDNS) — US duopoly ~70%+ of design software. Siemens EDA / Mentor (Siemens, SIE.DE) is #3. Export-control flashpoint: US ordered a halt on EDA sales to China May 2025, reversed early July 2025 after US-China trade talks. China domestic: Empyrean (301269.SZ), Primarius (688206.SH) — cannot match full-flow leading-edge coverage.

Arm Holdings (ARM) — dominant CPU instruction-set IP (all mobile + growing datacenter via Grace/Graviton); licenses into China via the Arm China JV (a control wrinkle). RISC-V open ISA rising as a license-risk hedge (SiFive private; Alibaba T-Head — China-favored). Interface IP: Synopsys, Cadence, Alphawave (AWE.L).
Cadence Palladium, Synopsys ZeBu — hardware emulation boxes; high-margin recurring revenue. Essential to tape out billion-transistor AI accelerators; demand scales with design complexity.

Done in-house by fabless leaders (Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Qualcomm) and hyperscalers. The high-value front-end where performance is won or lost; increasingly co-optimized with the target foundry process and packaging.

chain.r: RTL to mask-ready GDSII; turnkey backend
chain.d: ASIC design-service houses: Broadcom (AVGO), Marvell (MRVL), plus Taiwan turnkey backends Alchip (3661.TW) and GUC / Global Unichip (3443.TW, TSMC-affiliated); Socionext (Japan). Cross-link to Foundry — physical design is co-optimized with the process node.

chain.l: Interface IP — SerDes, HBM PHY, UCIe
chain.r: High-speed connectivity IP for AI chips
chain.d: Synopsys, Cadence, Alphawave Semi (AWE.L), Credo (CRDO). HBM PHY + UCIe chiplet-interconnect IP is the new bottleneck for accelerator design as compute disaggregates into chiplets. Astera Labs (ALAB) connectivity silicon adjacent.

chain.r: Physical masks transferring design to wafer
chain.d: Photronics (PLAB, US), Toppan (7911.T), DNP — Japan strong. EUV mask blanks: Hoya (7741.T) + AGC near-monopoly (Japan) — a hidden Japanese chokepoint upstream of every leading-edge fab.

EDA + IP sit at the head of Foundry, Memory, Accelerators, Optical and AR-SoC chains. License/royalty model = the most defensible and highest-margin layer of the entire universe; revenue compounds with design starts.

wfe.note: The most concentrated chokepoint in technology. ASML is the sole EUV maker; Zeiss optics + Cymer light source are single-source within it. US+NL+Japan jointly restrict advanced WFE to China. High-NA EUV (~$380M/tool) deploying first at Intel (14A); TSMC is notably holding back, sticking with 0.33-NA EUV + multipatterning for A14/A16.

chain.r: EUV / DUV scanners — the defining chokepoint
chain.d: ASML (ASML, Netherlands) — monopoly on EUV. High-NA EUV (EXE:5200, ~$380M): Intel lead-deployed first unit Dec 2025 for 14A; Samsung receiving for SF2/1.4nm; TSMC holding back (0.33-NA + multipatterning for A14/A16). DUV: ASML, Canon (7751.T), Nikon (7731.T). China SMEE far behind — immersion DUV under trial, sub-10nm scanners unlikely before ~2030.

Applied Materials (AMAT, ~18% WFE, broadest portfolio), Lam Research (LRCX — etch/deposition), KLA (KLAC — process control/metrology leader). Japan: Tokyo Electron (8035.T — coat/develop, etch), Screen (7735.T — cleaning), Hitachi High-Tech, Advantest (6857.T — test). Big-5 ~70% of WFE.

chain.r: EUV optics and lasers — single-source sub-chokepoints
chain.d: Carl Zeiss SMT (Germany, ASML-exclusive EUV optics — arguably the hardest-to-replicate component on earth). Light source: Cymer (ASML-owned, US — CO2-laser-pumped tin plasma) + Trumpf (Germany) lasers. No Zeiss + Cymer means no EUV. Pure single-source dependency.

VAT Group (VACN.SW — vacuum valves near-monopoly); MKS Instruments (MKSI); Advanced Energy (AEIS — RF power); Ichor (ICHR), Ultra Clean (UCTT) — subsystems/integration; Horiba (Japan — mass-flow controllers). Many single-source niches inside every tool.

Photoresist chokepoint: JSR, Tokyo Ohka (4186.T), Shin-Etsu, Fujifilm — Japan controls ~80-90% of advanced resist (a deliberate strategic lever). CMP slurry: Fujimi, CMC Materials. Cross-link to industrial gases / specialty materials in the Hard Assets map.

chain.r: Final integration of the fab tools
chain.d: ASML (Veldhoven NL), AMAT/LRCX (US), TEL/Screen (Japan). Thousands-deep supplier BOM; a single High-NA EUV system ships in dozens of crates and takes months to install — limiting how fast leading-edge capacity can scale.
High-margin recurring services + spares (AMAT CSBG, ASML installed-base management) ~30%+ of incumbent revenue — smooths the WFE capex cycle and deepens customer lock-in.

China stockpiled DUV ahead of curbs, driving mature-node overcapacity. Localization: NAURA (002371.SZ, ~#8 globally), AMEC (688012.SH — etch), Piotech (deposition) — but supplied <15% of internal demand. Lithography remains the unbridgeable gap.

WFE demand = the foundry/memory capex cycle. Buyers: TSMC, Samsung, Intel, Micron, SK Hynix, SMIC. ~$110B+/yr market; China was ~30-40% of spend during the localization rush. Cross-link to Foundry and Memory.

fdy.note: TSMC Fab 18 (Tainan) is the single most strategically important industrial site on earth — ~90% of leading-edge logic. N2 (2nm nanosheet) entered volume production Q4 2025; A16 (backside power) H2 2026. Taiwan concentration is the defining geopolitical risk of the AI era. SMIC caps China at ~7nm (DUV multipatterning) — the central US-China tech-war front.

chain.d: TSMC (2330.TW / TSM) leads: N3 ramping, N2 (nanosheet GAA) volume Q4 2025, A16 (backside power rail) H2 2026; record ~$50-56B 2026 capex. Samsung Foundry (005930.KS) SF2 GAA trailing on yield. Intel (INTC) 18A in production, 14A is its High-NA lead node. A 3-horse leading-edge race; TSMC has ~90%+ of <5nm.

Shin-Etsu (4063.T) + SUMCO (3436.T) Japan ~50%+ of 300mm wafers; GlobalWafers (6488.TW Taiwan), Siltronic (WAF.DE). Japan/Taiwan chokepoint. Cross-link to silicon / specialty materials in the Hard Assets map.

TSMC Fab 18 (Tainan) ~90% of leading-edge; Arizona fabs (N4/N3) ramping for geographic diversification. Samsung (Korea / Taylor TX). Intel (Oregon / Arizona). Taiwan concentration is the single largest tail risk in the AI supply chain.

chain.r: >16nm: power, analog, MCU, RF, display drivers
chain.d: UMC (2303.TW), GlobalFoundries (GFS), Tower (Israel), Vanguard (5347.TWO). China: SMIC (0981.HK), Hua Hong (1347.HK) — aggressive mature-node build-out raising Western overcapacity/dumping concerns. Display driver ICs and power discretes flow from here into devices and autos.

SMIC (0981.HK / 688981.SH) reached 7nm (N+2) without EUV via DUV multipatterning (low yield, high cost) — powers Huawei Ascend/Kirin. Reportedly piloting 5nm (press-sourced, treat as directional) at ~50% higher cost and ~1/3 TSMC yield. US controls cap China at ~7nm with no EUV access.

chain.r: Customers who design but do not fab
chain.d: Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), Apple, Qualcomm (QCOM), MediaTek (2454.TW), Broadcom (AVGO) — all depend on TSMC. Fabless + TSMC is the dominant model; IDM (Intel, Samsung) is the alternative. Cross-link to Accelerators.

Advantest (6857.T) + Teradyne (TER) duopoly in automated test equipment — demand surged with HBM/AI complexity. Probe cards: FormFactor (FORM), MJC (Japan). Yield ramp is the gating economic variable on every new node.

Leading-edge wafers flow to advanced packaging — increasingly done by TSMC itself (CoWoS/SoIC), blurring the foundry/OSAT line. CoWoS capacity is the gating bottleneck on AI GPU output. Cross-link to Advanced Packaging.

mem.note: HBM is THE supply gate on AI accelerator shipments — and a Korea-centric chokepoint. SK Hynix leads (~62% HBM, Q2 2025), Micron ~21% (overtook Samsung), Samsung ~17% and clawing back via Nvidia HBM4 qual. All three sold out for 2026. HBM4 (2026) adds a logic base die — pulling TSMC/foundry into the memory stack. China (CXMT, YMTC) years behind but closing on DDR5/NAND.

chain.r: DRAM cell scaling, 3D-NAND layers, HBM stacking
chain.d: DRAM oligopoly: Samsung (005930.KS), SK Hynix (000660.KS), Micron (MU) ~95% of DRAM. NAND adds Kioxia (Japan, ex-Toshiba), SanDisk (SNDK, WD spinoff), YMTC (China). DRAM scaling is slowing — HBM and 3D-DRAM are the frontier.

chain.r: Stacked DRAM for AI accelerators — the bottleneck
chain.d: SK Hynix (000660.KS) leads HBM3E (~62% share) and is Nvidia's lead supplier; Micron (MU) ~21% (overtook Samsung); Samsung ~17%, clearing Nvidia HBM4 qual. Each Blackwell GPU carries ~8 HBM stacks. HBM gates GPU output; all suppliers sold out for 2026. HBM4 (2026) integrates a logic base die fabbed at TSMC.

Korea (Samsung, SK Hynix) ~70% of DRAM; Micron (US/Taiwan/Japan/Singapore). China's CXMT (ChangXin, private) ramping DDR5-8000 / LPDDR5X — the fastest-growing threat, drawing US Entity-List scrutiny. Server DDR5 demand strong from AI hosts.
Samsung, SK Hynix (+Solidigm), Kioxia (Japan), SanDisk (SNDK), Micron, YMTC (China, 200+ layer, Entity-Listed). AI storage tier + enterprise SSD demand. More commoditized and cyclical than DRAM.

chain.r: SSD controllers, CXL, register clock drivers
chain.d: Phison (8299.TW), Silicon Motion (SIMO), Marvell, Microchip — controllers. CXL memory pooling emerging for AI servers. Montage (688008.SH China) DDR5 RCD/register clock drivers — a quiet China-exposed niche.

chain.r: TSV stacking, base die, MR-MUF / TC-bonding
chain.d: SK Hynix MR-MUF process advantage; TC-NCF (Samsung/Micron). TSV stacking + hybrid bonding for HBM4. Bonders: BESI (BESI.AS), ASMPT (0522.HK). Cross-link to Advanced Packaging — HBM4 base die fabbed at TSMC pulls foundry into memory.
DRAM makers + module houses: Kingston (private), ADATA (3260.TW), Smart Modular. Server RDIMM / MRDIMM for AI hosts; MRDIMM is the new high-bandwidth server-memory format.

chain.r: HBM to GPU, DDR5 to servers, NAND to storage
chain.d: HBM into accelerators; DDR5 into datacenter; LPDDR into edge-AI devices; NAND into storage. Memory is ~30%+ of an AI-server BOM and the swing factor in datacenter capex. Cross-link to Accelerators and DC Infrastructure.

pkg.note: The new Moore's-Law frontier as transistor scaling slows — value migrating from front-end to back-end. TSMC CoWoS is the single gating constraint on Nvidia/AMD AI GPU output; TSMC is doubling capacity yearly and outsourcing overflow to ASE/Amkor/SPIL. ABF substrates (Ibiden/Shinko/Unimicron) and hybrid-bonding tools (BESI) are the secondary chokepoints. Taiwan + China dominate OSAT.

chain.d: TSMC CoWoS (2.5D on silicon interposer) + SoIC (3D) + InFO; Intel Foveros/EMIB; Samsung I-Cube/X-Cube. Chiplet standard: UCIe. Advanced packaging is now ~20%+ of leading-edge device cost and rising.

chain.r: Interposer hosting GPU + HBM — THE bottleneck
chain.d: TSMC CoWoS is the gating constraint on AI GPU output; TSMC bringing on AP6/AP7 (Chiayi) and outsourcing overflow to ASE, Amkor, SPIL. Rubin Ultra reportedly re-architected over CoWoS-L yield. Panel-level packaging (FOPLP) is the next-gen escape from round-wafer interposer limits.

ASE Technology (3711.TW / ASX — world #1, ~45% of top-10), Amkor (AMKR — Korea-rooted, $7B Arizona fab w/ Apple/TSMC), JCET (600584.SH China #3), Powertech (6239.TW), KYEC (2449.TW — test), Tongfu/TFME (China). Taiwan + China dominate OSAT.

chain.r: ABF & BT substrates carrying the package
chain.d: ABF substrate chokepoint (top-5 ~74%): Unimicron (3037.TW, leader ~22%), Ibiden (4062.T), Shinko (6967.T Japan), Nan Ya PCB (8046.TW), AT&S (ATS.VI Austria). Ajinomoto (2802.T) makes the ABF film itself — a hidden near-monopoly. High-layer-count ABF for big AI dies is supply-constrained.

BE Semiconductor / BESI (BESI.AS) + Applied Materials lead hybrid bonding; ASMPT (0522.HK) bonders; Kulicke & Soffa (KLIC) wire/advanced. Hybrid bonding enables sub-10µm 3D stacking — the equipment chokepoint of advanced packaging.
Advantest (6857.T), Teradyne (TER) ATE; OSATs run test floors (KYEC, ASE). HBM/AI complexity lengthens test time, driving ATE demand. Burn-in: Aehr Test (AEHR).

chain.r: Underfill, molding compound, leadframes, bonding wire
chain.d: Resonac (ex-Showa Denko), Henkel, Namics — underfill; Sumitomo Bakelite — molding; leadframes ASMPT, Haesung. Bonding wire links to the precious-metals chain (Hard Assets map). Cross-link to specialty materials.
Output = finished accelerators/CPUs to system integrators. Back-end is where the AI compute supply chain is most physically constrained today. Cross-link to Accelerators and DC Infrastructure.

acc.note: Nvidia (~80-90% merchant AI silicon) on a Blackwell→Rubin cadence, defended by CUDA. The structural counter-trend is custom ASICs (Broadcom + Marvell co-design, ~95% of that market) for Google/AWS/Microsoft/Meta/OpenAI — projected to take ~28% of AI servers in 2026. The real bottlenecks sit upstream: TSMC CoWoS and SK Hynix HBM. China (Huawei Ascend) is fenced off from both.

chain.d: Nvidia (NVDA) — Hopper→Blackwell (GB200/GB300)→Vera Rubin (HBM4, 2026) cadence; CUDA software moat. AMD (AMD) MI355X shipping, MI400 series (2nm, HBM4) + Helios rack unveiled CES 2026. Intel Gaudi. All fabbed at TSMC; Nvidia ~80-90% of merchant AI training silicon.

chain.r: In-house accelerators to cut Nvidia dependence
chain.d: Google TPU (Broadcom-designed), AWS Trainium/Inferentia (Marvell + Alchip 3661.TW), Microsoft Maia (Marvell), Meta MTIA (Broadcom, through 2029), OpenAI (Broadcom, ~2027). Merchant ASIC duo Broadcom (AVGO) + Marvell (MRVL) ~95% of custom co-design; custom shipments projected +44.6% in 2026 (~28% of AI servers).

Huawei Ascend 910C (SMIC ~7nm, 96GB HBM2e; ~1/3 B200 BF16) — ~600K-unit 2026 target but realistically <300K given HBM scarcity (TSMC die-bank via Sophgo now exhausted). Cambricon (688256.SH), Biren, Moore Threads. China compute self-sufficiency is a national priority.

Every leading accelerator = TSMC leading-edge logic + CoWoS packaging + HBM. Designers are fabless; the true gates are upstream — TSMC CoWoS and SK Hynix HBM. Cross-link to Foundry, Packaging and Memory.

chain.r: GPU board, SXM module, OAM baseboard
chain.d: Nvidia SXM modules + GB200 superchip; HGX/MGX baseboards built by Taiwan ODMs (Foxconn 2317.TW, Quanta 2382.TW, Wistron). Astera Labs (ALAB) retimers on board. OAM is the open accelerator module standard for non-Nvidia silicon.
Nvidia DGX; NVL72 rack-scale (72 liquid-cooled GPUs) is the new unit of AI compute. Built by Super Micro (SMCI), Dell (DELL), HPE and Taiwan ODMs. Cross-link to DC Infrastructure and Networking.

CUDA is Nvidia's deepest moat — 15+ years of libraries. AMD ROCm, OpenAI Triton and hyperscaler stacks chip at it. Inference-optimization software (vLLM, TensorRT-LLM) is the fastest-growing layer as workloads shift training→inference.

chain.r: Hyperscalers, AI labs, enterprise, sovereign AI
chain.d: Buyers: Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, xAI, OpenAI, Oracle, plus neoclouds (CoreWeave, Nebius) and sovereign-AI clouds. ~$300-400B/yr datacenter capex is the demand engine for the whole stack. Cross-link to DC Infrastructure.

net.note: At 100k+ GPU scale the network gates cluster utilization — 'the network is the computer.' Broadcom is the quiet kingmaker (Tomahawk 6 'Davisson' 102.4 Tb/s, ~$10B AI switch backlog). The battlegrounds: Nvidia's proprietary NVLink scale-up vs open UALink, and InfiniBand vs Ethernet (Ultra Ethernet) scale-out. Signal-integrity silicon (Astera, Credo) is a fast-growing chokepoint at 224G.

chain.d: Broadcom (AVGO) Tomahawk 6 'Davisson' (102.4 Tb/s) + Jericho routing — dominant merchant switch silicon, ~$10B AI switch backlog; Nvidia Spectrum-X; Marvell (MRVL); Cisco Silicon One. Broadcom is the kingmaker of AI networking.

Nvidia NVLink/NVSwitch — proprietary, high-margin, locks in the GPU fabric. UALink (AMD, Broadcom et al.) is the open challenger. Scale-up bandwidth is the new competitive battleground; Nvidia took a ~$2B stake in Marvell around NVLink Fusion.

Nvidia Quantum InfiniBand vs Ethernet (Ultra Ethernet Consortium). Ethernet gaining for the largest clusters; Arista (ANET) the Ethernet leader for AI back-end networks; Nvidia pushing Spectrum-X. InfiniBand retains a training-cluster niche.

Astera Labs (ALAB) — Aries PCIe/CXL retimers, Scorpio fabric switches, Taurus AECs; the AI-connectivity pure-play, targeting UALink in 2026. Credo (CRDO) — ZeroFlap active electrical cables dominant top-of-rack. Marvell/Broadcom optical DSPs. Signal integrity is a growing chokepoint.

Arista (ANET), Cisco (CSCO), Nvidia, Juniper (now HPE). White-box for hyperscalers built by Taiwan ODMs: Accton (2345.TW), Quanta, Foxconn; Celestica (CLS) ODM networking. White-box is the cost-down path for hyperscale fabrics.
Nvidia BlueField DPU + ConnectX NIC; AMD Pensando; Intel IPU. Networking is moving into the server to feed GPUs directly and offload host CPUs — a fast-growing silicon socket.
Pluggable optical transceivers today; co-packaged optics (CPO) emerging. Optics is increasingly the power/bandwidth bottleneck of AI networking. Cross-link to Optical & Photonics.

Buyers: hyperscalers + neoclouds. Networking is ~10-15% of AI datacenter capex and rising as cluster sizes grow. Network topology and bandwidth directly cap effective compute. Cross-link to Accelerators and DC Infrastructure.

dci.note: Taiwan ODMs (Foxconn, Quanta, Wiwynn ~50% of hyperscale AI-server procurement) build the world's AI servers. The binding constraint has shifted from chips to POWER and COOLING: liquid cooling jumps from ~15% (2024) to ~76% of the mix by 2026 (GB200 mandates it), CDU capacity is the cooling bottleneck, and grid access + transformers gate the buildout. Deepest cross-link to the energy supply chain.

chain.d: Taiwan dominates: Foxconn/Hon Hai (2317.TW), Quanta (2382.TW), Wiwynn (6669.TW — ~50% of hyperscale AI-server procurement), Wistron (3231.TW), Inventec (2356.TW), Gigabyte (2376.TW). US: Super Micro (SMCI), Dell (DELL), HPE. Cross-link to Accelerators (rack-scale integration).

Delta Electronics (2308.TW — world-leading power supplies), Lite-On (2301.TW), Monolithic Power (MPWR — power management), Infineon, Vicor (VICR). 48V-to-chip power for 1kW+ GPUs is a fast-evolving bottleneck; PSU lead times stretched.

Liquid-cooling inflection (~15% 2024 → ~76% of mix by 2026): Vertiv (VRT), Boyd, Asetek (ASTK.OL); Taiwan: Auras (3324.TWO), AVC / Asia Vital Components (3017.TW), Cooler Master. CDU (coolant distribution unit) capacity is the key bottleneck — market ~$1B→$7.7B. GB200 mandates liquid cooling.

Vertiv (VRT), Eaton (ETN), Schneider, ABB, Legrand. Rack densities hitting 100-130kW+ reshape distribution. Transformer + switchgear lead times are multi-year. Cross-link to the grid/electrical chain in the Hard Assets map.

Hyperscaler self-build + colo: Digital Realty (DLR), Equinix (EQIX); developers Vantage, QTS; neoclouds CoreWeave (CRWV). Prefab modular DC. The constraint is increasingly power-grid access and permitting, not capital.

chain.r: Gas turbines, nuclear PPA, backup gen
chain.d: Power availability is the #1 constraint on the AI buildout (DCs ~4% of US electricity in 2026). Gas turbines (GE Vernova GEV), behind-the-meter gas, nuclear SMR/PPAs; generators Cummins (CMI), Caterpillar (CAT). The deepest cross-link into the energy supply chain (Hard Assets map).
DCIM, PUE optimization, GPU scheduling. Nvidia + hyperscaler software stacks. Less directly investable but central to utilization economics and effective cost per token.

Hyperscaler capex (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META) + neoclouds (CoreWeave, Nebius, Crusoe) + sovereign AI. ~$300-400B/yr is the demand engine for the entire AI hardware stack. Cross-link to Accelerators and Networking.

opt.note: One of the highest-growth AI hardware segments — optics content per GPU is rising sharply (800G→1.6T). The deepest chokepoint is EML lasers (<5 makers; Nvidia committed ~$4B to lock Lumentum + Coherent supply, lead times past 2027). China dominates module assembly (~63% global; InnoLight + Eoptolink ~60% of Nvidia 800G), but US silicon (Marvell/Broadcom DSPs) + lasers capture the value. CPO (TSMC COUPE) is the 2026 inflection.

chain.d: Indium-phosphide and silicon-photonics design. Coherent (COHR), Lumentum (LITE), Marvell (Inphi), Broadcom. Vertically integrated laser/chip makers hold the edge as line rates climb to 1.6T and beyond.

Fewer than five firms make EMLs at scale: Lumentum (LITE), Coherent (COHR), Mitsubishi Electric (6503.T), Sumitomo (Japan), Broadcom. Nvidia committed ~$4B (~$2B each to Lumentum + Coherent) in 2026 to lock laser supply; EML lead times stretch past 2027 for others. The deepest optical chokepoint.

chain.r: The chip that drives the optics
chain.d: Marvell (MRVL) + Broadcom (AVGO) dominate PAM4 optical DSPs — the brains of a transceiver — moving to 200G/lane. Linear-drive optics (LPO) and CPO threaten to disrupt the DSP socket. This is where US silicon captures value inside China-assembled modules.

China dominates assembly (~63% global): InnoLight (300308.SZ — world #1), Eoptolink (300502.SZ) — together ~60% of Nvidia 800G; Accelink (002281.SZ). Contract manufacturer Fabrinet (FN, Thailand) builds for Nvidia/Cisco/Coherent (first $1B quarter). Module assembly is a quiet China geopolitical exposure.

chain.r: Optics moved next to the switch/GPU die
chain.d: The 2026 inflection: Nvidia Quantum-X (InfiniBand) + Spectrum-X (Ethernet) on TSMC COUPE silicon-photonics packaging; Broadcom Tomahawk 6 'Davisson' CPO. Cuts networking power sharply. TSMC is the silicon-photonics packaging chokepoint. Cross-link to Packaging and Foundry.
Corning (GLW) fiber + connectivity; US Conec, Senko — connectors; Taiwan/China cabling. AI clusters need massive intra-DC fiber. Cross-link to fiber-optic glass (materials) in the Hard Assets map.

chain.r: Optical circuit switching for AI pods
chain.d: Google's MEMS-based OCS in TPU pods cuts network cost/power at scale. Niche but strategically interesting as cluster sizes grow; a structural alternative to all-electrical fabrics.

Optics content per GPU is rising sharply (multiple 800G/1.6T ports per accelerator); 800G+ shipments ~24M (2025) → ~63M (2026), AI optical market ~$16.5B→$26B. Buyers: hyperscalers via switch/server vendors. Cross-link to Networking.

act.note: Humanoids create a new BOM of ~28-40 actuators per robot. The costliest precision parts are chokepoints historically owned by Japan: harmonic/strain-wave reducers (Harmonic Drive 6324.T), cycloidal/RV reducers (Nabtesco ~60% share), planetary roller screws (NSK/THK/GSA). China is aggressively localizing all three to cut cost — Green Harmonic, Sanhua (exclusive Tier-1 Optimus assembler), Inovance — and Tesla Optimus Gen-3 is reportedly ~70% China-sourced.

chain.d: Humanoid programs drive the new BOM (~28-40 actuators/robot): Tesla Optimus, Figure AI, Agility, Apptronik, Boston Dynamics (Hyundai 005380.KS), plus China — Unitree (IPO expected 2026; G1 ~$16K), UBTech (9880.HK), AgiBot, Fourier. Sanhua Intelligent (002050.SZ) is exclusive Tier-1 Optimus actuator assembler ($685M order Oct 2025). Cross-link to Sensing and Accelerators (the robot brain).

chain.r: High-ratio gearing for robot joints — chokepoint
chain.d: Harmonic Drive Systems (6324.T) — Japan's historic near-monopoly in strain-wave reducers, designed into Western humanoids. China localizing fast: Green Harmonic / Suzhou (688017.SH, ~25%+ China share, targeting Optimus supply via a 500K-unit/yr plant), Leaderdrive (Shenzhen-listed), Shenzhen Han's. The reducer is the costliest part of a robot joint.

Nabtesco (6268.T) ~60% of precision RV reducers for mid-large industrial joints; Sumitomo Heavy (6302.T) Fine Cyclo. China: Zhongda Leader, Qinchuan. Japanese dominance here mirrors its strongholds in WFE chemicals and precision optics.

chain.r: Linear actuation for limbs — humanoid bottleneck
chain.d: Planetary roller screws are an Optimus-driven bottleneck: GSA (Germany, primary Optimus supplier), NSK (6471.T), THK (6481.T), Rollvis (Swiss), Ewellix (Schaeffler SHA.DE). China second-sourcing: Hangzhou Xinjian, Wuzhou Xinchun (Thailand plant ~2026). Few makers can produce at humanoid volume/cost.

Frameless torque motors: Kollmorgen / Allied Motion (now Regal Rexnord RRX), Nidec (6594.T), Moog. China motion-control: Inovance (300124.SZ — vertically integrating motors, drives, screws for humanoids), Estun (002747.SZ), Leadshine. Inovance is China's motion-control champion scaling into humanoids.

Encoders: Renishaw (RSW.L), Heidenhain (Germany), Nidec-Tosok. Servo drives: Yaskawa (6506.T), Inovance, Estun. Precision encoders and joint torque sensing are an under-appreciated robotics chokepoint enabling compliant control.

The 'Big Four': Fanuc (6954.T), Yaskawa (6506.T) — Japan; ABB (ABBN.SW); KUKA (Midea 000333.SZ — China-owned). China's Estun, Inovance, JAKA, Efort rising fast in cobots/industrial — domestic share climbing.

Tesla Optimus, Figure, Unitree (cost leader), UBTech, Agility. China's vertically integrated, low-cost actuator supply chain is the key competitive variable — Optimus Gen-3 reportedly ~70% China-sourced. Cross-link to Sensing/Vision and the AI compute 'brain'.

sen.note: The robot 'brain' (Nvidia Jetson Thor, GA 2025) plus embodied-AI foundation models (Nvidia GR00T, Physical Intelligence) link robotics to AI accelerators. Sensing chokepoints: Sony CMOS image sensors (Japan), China-led low-cost lidar (Hesai >1.6M units 2025), and an unsolved gap in force/torque + tactile sensing critical for dexterous manipulation.

chain.r: Edge AI inference for embodied robots
chain.d: Nvidia Jetson Thor (Blackwell, ~2,070 FP4 TFLOPS, GA 2025 — adopted by Unitree, UBTech, AgiBot); Qualcomm robotics; hyperscaler edge. Embodied-AI foundation models: Nvidia GR00T, Physical Intelligence, Skild. The brain links robotics to AI Accelerators.

Image sensors: Sony (6758.T — dominant CMOS), Omnivision (Will Semi 603501.SH), onsemi (ON). Industrial cameras: Cognex (CGNX), Basler, Keyence (6861.T — Japan sensor powerhouse). Sony's sensor lead is a Japanese chokepoint.

China cost leaders: Hesai (HSAI — >1.6M units 2025, Nvidia robotics partner), RoboSense (2498.HK); US: Ouster (OUST), Luminar (LAZR — auto-focused, stressed). Structured-light/ToF depth: Orbbec (688322.SH). China dominates low-cost lidar — a fast-commoditizing layer.

6-axis F/T sensors: ATI (Novanta NOVT), Bota Systems, Sunrise Instruments (China). Tactile / e-skin is an emerging gap critical for dexterous manipulation — few scaled suppliers, mostly startups. A genuine humanoid bottleneck.

Bosch (private), TDK InvenSense (6762.T — ICM-42688 widely used in humanoids), STMicro (STM), Analog Devices (ADI). MEMS IMUs ride consumer-electronics scale; balance/locomotion quality depends on them.
Overlaps Actuators — magnetic/optical encoders (Renishaw, AMS, Heidenhain). Joint torque sensing is key to safe, compliant control and is co-designed with the actuator.

chain.r: Turning sensor data into world models
chain.d: VSLAM, perception stacks, sim-to-real training (Nvidia Isaac / Omniverse). The software that makes sensing useful — increasingly bundled with the compute platform and the foundation model.
Humanoids, AMRs (warehouse), surgical/medical robotics (Intuitive ISRG). Sensing quality gates real-world deployment. Cross-link to Actuators and the AI compute brain.

dsp.note: The make-or-break component for AR glasses: tiny, ultra-bright, high-PPI near-eye displays. No clear winner among microLED (brightest, lowest yield), OLEDoS (premium MR), and LCoS (mature/cheap for waveguides). Sony OLEDoS powers Apple Vision Pro; LCoS (Omnivision/Himax) is inside the Meta Ray-Ban Display; full-color microLED (JBD) is the long-term endgame. Goertek (China) dominates light-engine assembly.

chain.d: The hardest AR bottleneck — tiny, bright, high-PPI displays. Competing tech: microLED (brightest, hardest to make), OLED-on-silicon (OLEDoS), LCoS, laser beam scanning. No clear winner yet; the make-or-break component alongside the waveguide.

JBD / Jade Bird Display (China — dominant AR microLED, 0.3in monochrome, full-color sampling), Porotech (UK), AUO (2409.TW), Playnitride (Taiwan), ams OSRAM (AMS.SW), Raysolve (China). Mass-transfer/efficiency yield is unsolved — brightest option for daylight AR but lowest yield. Taiwan + China + UK lead.

Sony (6758.T — Apple Vision Pro supplier; 1.3in 4K, 0.44in FHD panels), BOE (000725.SZ China — ~400K-nit microLED in 2025 portfolio), SeeYa (China), eMagin (Samsung Display). Korea/Japan/China display muscle; powers premium MR headsets.

Himax (HIMX — high-contrast LCoS), Omnivision (Will Semi 603501.SH — LCoS panel inside Meta Ray-Ban Display), Compound Photonics, MicroVision (MVIS — laser beam scanning). LCoS is mature and lower-cost for waveguide AR; Himax is a Taiwan-listed pure-play exposure.

The integrated module that injects light into the waveguide. Goertek (002241.SZ) built the Ray-Ban Display projection engine — a key China assembly chokepoint. Miniaturization + efficiency is the system-level challenge.
Himax (HIMX), Novatek (3034.TW), Raydium (3592.TWO) — Taiwan dominates display driver ICs. Cross-link to Foundry (driver ICs fabbed at UMC/TSMC mature nodes).
microLED/OLEDoS sit on a CMOS backplane fabbed at foundries (TSMC, UMC, X-Fab). Links the display chain back into the semiconductor stack. Cross-link to Foundry.
Apple Vision Pro, Meta Quest, Meta Orion (microLED), smart glasses. Display is the gating cost/quality component. Cross-link to AR Optics & Systems — the waveguide the display feeds.

arx.note: Getting the image to the eye in a glasses form factor is the second make-or-break problem. Waveguide combiners (Lumus reflective — in the $799 Meta Ray-Ban Display; diffractive Dispelix/DigiLens) suffer low light-efficiency and yield. High-index glass (Schott, Corning, Ohara) and emerging SiC waveguides (index ~2.7, used in Meta Orion) are deep material chokepoints. Qualcomm is the de-facto AR SoC; Goertek/Luxshare (China) dominate assembly.

chain.r: Waveguide vs birdbath vs pancake design
chain.d: How to deliver the image in a glasses form factor: diffractive/reflective waveguides (AR), pancake optics (VR/MR), birdbath (cheaper AR). Waveguide efficiency + manufacturability is the core unsolved AR problem alongside displays.

chain.r: The lens overlaying digital on the real world
chain.d: Reflective/geometric: Lumus (Israel — inside the $799 Meta Ray-Ban Display, launched Sept 2025). Diffractive: Dispelix (Finland, polymer waveguides w/ Mitsui Chemicals), DigiLens, WaveOptics (Snap-owned), Vuzix (VUZI — OEM maker, acquired ex-Google waveguide lab 2025). Low efficiency, rainbow artifacts and yield make this a deep chokepoint.

chain.r: The glass/SiC the waveguide is etched into
chain.d: High-refractive-index glass: Corning (GLW), Schott (Germany — Ray-Ban Display waveguide glass), Ohara (5218.T), Hoya (7741.T). Emerging SiC waveguides (index ~2.7, wide FOV) used in Meta Orion — suppliers Coherent (COHR), SICC / Tianyue (688234.SH); no mature commercial SiC supply chain yet. Cross-link to specialty glass (materials).
Nanoimprint lithography for surface-relief gratings: EV Group, Canon (nanoimprint), SUSS MicroTec. Wafer-scale waveguide manufacturing yield is the cost gate; overlaps WFE tooling.
Qualcomm Snapdragon AR/XR (dominant), Apple silicon, MediaTek. Low-power on-device AI for glasses is the constraint. Qualcomm is the de-facto AR SoC chokepoint. Cross-link to Accelerators / edge AI.
Eye-tracking (Tobii), SLAM cameras (Sony sensors), depth, microphones, hand-tracking. The sensor suite drives the context-aware value of smart glasses. Cross-link to Sensing & Vision.

Meta (Ray-Ban + Orion), Apple (Vision Pro), Google/Samsung (Android XR), Snap Spectacles. China brands: Xreal (~28% video-glasses share), Rokid, INMO, RayNeo (TCL). Assembly: Goertek (002241.SZ), Luxshare (002475.SZ), Pegatron — China dominates device assembly. VR pancake optics: Goertek, Young Optics.

Meta Ray-Ban breakout (audio/camera AI glasses) is the consumer on-ramp toward full AR; enterprise AR (Vuzix, RealWear). Display + waveguide + SoC are the gating chokepoints. Cross-link to Micro-displays.
